subject: UX & Research
title: Feature Adoption Rate
description: A fast breakdown of Feature Adoption Rate for builders.

hook.text: You ship a feature. 8% of users try it. Is that good? Pendo found that average feature adoption is just 5%.

buildup.text: Feature adoption = users who use a feature ÷ total active users. Most features are used by almost nobody.

discovery.text: Low adoption isn't always a failure. A feature for power users won't hit 50%. Context sets the target.

twist.text: Features nobody uses still cost maintenance. Track adoption to decide what to invest in and what to kill.

climax.text: Improve adoption with better discovery: tooltips, onboarding prompts, and contextual feature nudges.

punchline.text: Most features are ignored. Track adoption to focus investment.

quiz.question: What did Pendo find about average feature adoption in SaaS products?
options: Average adoption is only about 5% — most features go largely unused
Most features reach 60% adoption within the first week
Only enterprise features have low adoption rates
